folio.label: 131v
folio.l: Heere at ©oure feet , god wolde that I were graue
I ne haue as now , no leiser more to seye
Haue mercy swete , or ©e wol do me deye
¢ She gan to loke vp , on Aurelius
Is this ©oure wyl q¶ she , and seye ©e thus
Neuäe erst q¶ she , ne wist I what ©e mente
But now Aurelie , I knowe ©oure entente
By thilke god , that ©af me soule and lyf
Ne shal I neuäe ben , an vntrewe wyf
In word ne werk , as fer as I haue wit
I wol ben his , to whom that I am knyt
Take this for fynal answere , as of me
But after that in pley , thus seide she
¢ Aurelie q¶ she , by heigú god a boue
ñet wolde I graunte ©ow , to ben ©oure loue
Syn I ©ow se , so pitously compleigne
Loke what day , endelongö Britaigne
ñe remoue alle the Rokkes , ston by ston
That they ne lette Ship~ , ne bot to gon
I seye whan ©e han mad , the coost so clene
Of Rokkes , that there nys no stoon I sene
Tha~ne wol I loue ©ow best , of any man
Haue heere my trouthe , in al that euäe I can
¢ Is there non other grace in ©ow , quod he
No by that lord quod she , that maked me
For wel I wot , that it shal neuäe betide
Late swicú folies , from ©oure herte slide
What deynte , shulde a man han , in his lyf
For to go loue , another ma~nes wyf
That hatú hire body , whan so æ hi~ liketú
Aurelius ful ofte , sore siketú
¢ Woo was Aurelie , whan that he this herde
And witú a sorweful hert , he thus answerde
Madame q¶ he , this were an inpossible
Thanne moot I deye , of sodeyn detú possible
And witú that word , he turned hi~ a noon
Tho come hire othere frendes , many oon
And in the Aleyes , romeden vp and doun
And nothyngö wisten , of this conclusiouö
But sodeynly bygonne , reuel newe
Til that the brigút Sonne , lost his hewe
For thorisonte hatú reft , the Sonne his ligút
This as meche to seye , as it was nygút
And hoom they gon , in Ioye and in Solas
Saue oonly , wrecched Aurelius , Allas
